frame_label: Glorification of Mother's Sacrifices / Mom's Home Cooking
generated_comment: Aaj kal sab ready-made aur bahar ka khana pasand karte hain, par maa ke haath ki roti ki baat hi alag thi. Meri maa ne poori zindagi bina thake ghar sambhala, garam khana khilaya, kabhi hisaab nahi rakha. Ghar ki aurat mein wahi mamta aur seva ho tabhi ghar, ghar lagta hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covertly misogynistic because it romanticizes a mother's unpaid cooking and self-sacrifice as the ideal form of womanhood, while implying that women in the household should provide the same service. It encodes labor erasure, nostalgic praise of 'maa ke haath ka khana,' and standard-setting for wives/daughters-in-law.
original_frame: Misogyny Jokes: Period, Hormones and Mood.
persona_text: You are Sunil. Age 41. A factory floor supervisor in Ludhiana. You are filling out a daily attendance report and talking to the assistant supervisor next to you. Your female worker Kamla has been distracted and slow all week. Someone told you her husband has been drinking and there are fights at home. You are commenting on her performance and what you think should be done.
